SIPP-IHT WINDOW · 10 MONTHS LEFT
From 6 April 2027 — in ~10 months — unused pensions enter your estate for Inheritance Tax (IHT). Planning options collapse after this date.

drawers.allowances:
←
Back
Allowances — what's left to use
ALLOWANCES — WHAT'S LEFT TO USE
62% of your allowances still available
Pension annual allowance
Carries forward 3 yrs
This year: we don’t have your pension contributions yet — your full £60k allowance may be available.
Carried forward (last 3 years): not yet known — add your last three years’ pension contributions to see what you can still use.
Unlike your ISA, dividend and capital-gains allowances — which reset and are lost each 5 April — unused pension allowance carries forward three tax years. Add your pension details to turn this estimate into your real figures.
ISA (tax-free savings)
£11k / £20k
Personal savings allowance (PSA)
£0 / £500
Capital gains tax-free amount
£0 / £3k
Dividend allowance
£500 / £500
Personal allowance
£0 / £13k
Cash-ISA £12k cap (announced) · From 6 Apr 2027 the amount you can hold in a cash ISA is set to fall to £12,000 for under-65s; the remaining £8,000 of your £20,000 ISA allowance would need to go into a stocks-and-shares ISA, an innovative-finance ISA, or a Lifetime ISA instead.
Detail ›
Information and guidance only. Not personal advice. Verify decisions with a qualified FCA-authorised adviser before acting.

Effective rate 18.5% on £38,000 held in a general investment account (GIA)

£500 / £500
Basic 10.75%
Higher 35.75%
Add'l 39.35%
Tax paid
£7k
